Title: Structural insights into the catalytic mechanism of the phenylethanoid glycoside rhamnosyltransferase UGT79G15 from Rehmannia glutinosa

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Rehmannia glutinosa enzyme characterized in vitro / heterologous protein expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a expression system for recombinant protein production
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Osmanthuside A 1,3-rhamnosylation step in PhG pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; plant secondary product glycosyltransferase (PSPG) family; GT-B fold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Osmanthuside A; forsythiaside A (mimic acceptor); UDP-rhamnose
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Osmanthuside B from osmanthuside A; poliumoside from forsythiaside A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to the 3′-OH of the central glucose moiety of phenylethanoid glycosides, converting osmanthuside A to osmanthuside B with strict 1,3-regiospecificity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key intermediate / immediate pathway intermediate toward downstream phenylethanoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key intermediate-forming step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylethanoid glycosides (with osmanthuside B as key intermediate)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Important medicinal natural products with remarkable pharmacological properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Medicinal plant tissues; specific organ not stated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of phenylethanoid glycosides by converting osmanthuside A to the key intermediate osmanthuside B; engineered I204W variant increases osmanthuside A conversion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays at 30°C; donor specificity tests with alternative UDP-sugars; crystallography with forsythiaside A as stable mimic acceptor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures of apo/UDP/UDP-forsythiaside A complexes; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assays; kinetic analysis; HPLC and LC-HRMS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for synthetic biology and metabolic engineering of phenylethanoid glycosides; residue I204 is engineering hotspot, and I204W improved catalytic efficiency 2.2-fold for osmanthuside A conversion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: I204W enhancement was substrate-dependent and did not improve activity toward bulkier forsythiaside A; many pocket mutations reduced activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79G15 is an osmanthuside A 1,3-rhamnosyltransferase from Rehmannia glutinosa that specifically uses UDP-rhamnose to produce osmanthuside B, a key intermediate in phenylethanoid glycoside biosynthesis. 2. Crystal structures and mutagenesis defined the catalytic H21-D118 dyad and donor-recognition residues including V139, Q277, and Q377, as well as a distinctive funnel-shaped acceptor pocket. 3. Structure-guided engineering identified I204W as an improved variant with a ~2.2-fold higher catalytic efficiency toward osmanthuside A due to reduced Km.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The enzyme shows strict donor specificity for UDP-rhamnose; no activity detected with UDP-glucose, UDP-xylose, UDP-arabinose, UDP-N-acetylglucosamine, or UDP-glucuronic acid. The 4′-hydroxycinnamoyl group position is critical for acceptor recognition. Forsythiaside A was used as a structural mimic because osmanthuside A is unstable in buffer.
